summary: Verified the /checkout guest-validation fix. With a valid Stripe card entered but passenger fields (fullName/email/phone) left empty, clicking the Pay submit button now correctly triggers validateForm(), displays 3 'Requis' errors, and blocks ALL backend calls (setup-intent, register, login, cards). Filling all three fields then submitting proceeds to the backend flow (auth/register + auth/login + booking/setup-intent fired). Code review of handleSubmit (line 508: `if (!isAuthenticated && !validateForm()) return;` placed BEFORE the `if (verifiedCardId) return handlePay();` shortcut) and WalletPaymentButton onPaymentMethod auto-fill (lines 943-951, only fills empty fields via `prev.fullName || bd.name`) both confirmed present and correct.
critical_code_review_comments: handleSubmit line 508 correctly runs validateForm() at the TOP for guests before verifiedCardId shortcut — fix is in the right place. No regression path bypasses validation.
WalletPaymentButton onPaymentMethod (lines 943-951) auto-fills form.fullName/email/phone from pm.billing_details using `prev.x || bd.x` pattern — correctly avoids overwriting user-typed input.
Checkout.js is 1215 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Consider splitting UnifiedCheckoutForm, WalletPaymentButton, and Passenger form into separate modules for maintainability. Not blocking.
Submit button disabled logic (line 1015) is complex and repeats `!verifiedCardId` twice; could be extracted into a memoized `canSubmit` variable for readability.
seed_data_creation: Seeded sessionStorage['zont_booking'] with searchData + selectedCar to reach /checkout as guest.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Booking context is stored in sessionStorage under key 'zont_booking' (JSON with searchData, selectedCar, _ts). The /checkout submit button is disabled until Stripe CardElement is complete OR verifiedCardId is set. To simulate the exact original bug (verifiedCardId set via Apple/Google Pay, then Pay clicked without form filled), typing test card 4242424242424242 into the Stripe iframe enables the button and reproduces the code path guarded by the fix. With empty passenger fields and a valid card, submit now shows 3 'Requis' errors and fires zero network calls — confirming the shortcut cannot bypass validation.
rca of the issue: Original bug: handleSubmit had `if (verifiedCardId) return handlePay();` executed before validateForm(). Any user whose card had already been verified (either via wallet/Apple Pay or a prior 3DS step) could resubmit with the passenger form completely empty and a booking payload would fire with blank name/email/phone. Fix: moved `if (!isAuthenticated && !validateForm()) return;` to line 508, BEFORE the verifiedCardId shortcut. Additionally, WalletPaymentButton.onPaymentMethod now auto-populates form.fullName/email/phone from Stripe's pm.billing_details so wallet-only users still have identity data. Verified by Playwright: empty submit with a complete card → 3 Requis errors and 0 backend calls.
